title: Meditations (Meric Casaubon translation)
description: Marcus Aurelius's private philosophical notebook, 12 books. Primary source for his decision-making principles, value hierarchy, and Stoic framework. Public domain Casaubon translation from Project Gutenberg.

title: Historia Augusta: Life of Marcus Antoninus
description: Sections 1-29 covering Marcus's full biography: birth, education, Stoic training, adoption, co-rule with Verus, the Marcomannic and Parthian wars, administrative reforms, the Avidius Cassius revolt, and death. Public domain Loeb-era translation.

title: Cassius Dio, Roman History — Epitome of Books 71-72
description: Contemporaneous historian's account of Marcus's reign, covering the German wars, barbarian diplomacy, the Rain Miracle, the Avidius Cassius revolt, and character assessment. Loeb 1927 translation. Public domain.

title: Fronto-Marcus Aurelius Correspondence: Synthesized Context
description: Synthesized context file covering key incidents from the Fronto-Marcus letters (c. 138-166 CE): the rhetoric-vs-philosophy decision, self-criticism practices, Lorium retreat letters, Verus campaign correspondence, and structural insights. Based on Haines Loeb 1919 (public domain).
